source.title: Pain and emotion interactions in subregions of the cingulate gyrus

confidence_rationale: Cingulate-substrate anatomy is the single most important scoping input for the new SD cluster. Vogt's four-region parcellation (ACC / MCC / PCC / RSC) with distinct cytoarchitecture and distinct pain-response profiles is the canonical anatomical frame. Very high source quality, very high mapping fidelity for 'where to put the substrate,' moderate transfer risk because some human/primate differences exist.
failure_signatures: Modelling the cingulate as a single homogeneous region will collapse functions that Vogt shows are anatomically and computationally distinct: pain affect (pACC/sgACC), effortful action selection (aMCC), motor preparation (pMCC), and memory/attention (PCC). A single 'z_cingulate' latent will not recover the three-way partition experiments require.

The pACC/sgACC role in visceral/autonomic modulation of pain means that a substrate missing endocrine/autonomic efferents will not reproduce the 'suffering amplifies pain sensitivity' phenomenon the biology predicts -- harm_rate will be externally dictated, not internally regulated.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The cingulate gyrus is not a unitary structure. It comprises four cytoarchitecturally and functionally distinct regions: anterior cingulate cortex (ACC; further split into perigenual pACC and subgenual sgACC) for emotional/affective processing and autonomic regulation; midcingulate cortex (MCC; split into anterior aMCC and posterior pMCC) for action-outcome evaluation and motor control; posterior cingulate cortex (PCC) for memory/orientation; and retrosplenial cortex (RSC) for spatial-contextual memory. Pain affect, effortful action selection, motor engagement, and contextual retrieval each live in a different subregion.
mapping.ree_translation: The new SD cluster (cingulate integration substrate) cannot be a single module. It needs to parallel the anatomy: a pACC-like module for affective-pain -> autonomic/valence coupling, an aMCC-like module for effort-value and action-outcome credit assignment, a pMCC-like module for motor gating (this is adjacent to MECH-090 beta propagation gate), and a PCC-like module for offline/contextual retrieval (adjacent to INV-049 sleep and MECH-092 micro-quiescence replay). The z_harm_a consumer question then becomes specifically an aMCC-domain question, while the urgency-interrupt role (Option C) is more an AIC-pACC question.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Vogt's parcellation is based on macaque and human cytoarchitecture; the functional boundaries are sharper anatomically than they are computationally. ree-v3 does not need to recapitulate four separate modules -- it needs four separable *computations* that can share substrate. Treating the anatomy as a hard architectural constraint would be over-fitting to biology.
mapping.source_context: Comprehensive anatomical and functional review; cytoarchitecture + tract-tracing in macaque, lesion and fMRI in humans, with pain-specific focus.

confidence_components.notes: Vogt is the canonical anatomical reference for cingulate parcellation; the review has held up against later work. Moderate transfer risk because the anatomical parcellation doesn't translate one-to-one into separable computations in a unified latent architecture -- ree-v3 can in principle compute all four functions in one module if the signals are separable.
